working_tree: clean implementation commit during final profiling
compatibility_protocol: A separate same-revision pass used one warm-up and three measured runs per level with --verify-openpyxl; all twelve representative outputs opened. The runner also required exact compressed-payload equality for every planned raw-copy entry in all outputs.
protocol: Each performance profile generated a fresh DEFLATE level 6 source workbook, ran two warm-ups, retained five measured process runs per case, and selected the elapsed-time median representative. Preliminary cold/scan-affected performance values are excluded.
resource_metrics: Output-plan copied/rewritten/omitted entry lists, raw compressed-copy entry names/count/bytes, single-pass transform telemetry, and benchmark-process peak working set
raw_copy_scope: Production minizip-ng output only; unchanged source entries are eligible when source and requested output compression methods match. DEFLATE levels may differ because raw-copy preserves the source compressed payload. Rewritten entries are encoded at the requested level.
scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, one one-million-cell numeric source workbook, four Patch scenarios, and DEFLATE levels 1/3/6. No cross-machine or arbitrary-workload claim.
